Package cc.quaternion.potshaper

Class PotFill

java.lang.Object
cc.quaternion.potshaper.PotFill
public class PotFill extends Object
Represents a fill style for a shape or drawing area, supporting solid colors, gradients, images, and patterns.

This class is immutable and provides factory methods for creating different types of fills. Each fill type is defined by a PotFill.Type and carries associated data such as color, gradient, image bytes, or pattern specification. The fill can also have an opacity value applied uniformly.

Usage Example


 // Create a solid red fill with 50% opacity
 PotFill redFill = PotFill.solid(PotColor.RED, 0.5);

 // Create a gradient fill
 PotGradient grad = PotGradient.linear(...);
 PotFill gradientFill = PotFill.gradient(grad);

 // Create a pattern fill
 PotFill patternFill = PotFill.pattern(PatternType.CROSS, PotColor.BLACK, PotColor.WHITE);
Since:
1.0
Author:
Quaternion8192

    Description
    static enum 
    PotFill.PatternType
    Defines the pattern type for a pattern fill, corresponding to OpenXML preset patterns.
    static enum 
    PotFill.Type
    Defines the type of fill.

  • Field Details

    • NONE

      public static final PotFill NONE
      A fill representing no fill (transparent).

    • WHITE

      public static final PotFill WHITE
      A solid white fill.

    • BLACK

      public static final PotFill BLACK
      A solid black fill.

    • TRANSPARENT

      public static final PotFill TRANSPARENT
      A solid transparent fill.

  • Method Details

    • solid

      public static PotFill solid(PotColor color)
      Creates a solid fill with the specified color and full opacity.

      The resulting fill will have PotFill.Type.SOLID and an opacity of 1.0.

      Parameters:
      color - the color of the fill
      Returns:
      a solid fill with the given color
      See Also:
      • solid(PotColor, double)

    • solid

      public static PotFill solid(PotColor color, double opacity)
      Creates a solid fill with the specified color and opacity.

      The resulting fill will have PotFill.Type.SOLID. The opacity is clamped to the range [0.0, 1.0].

      Parameters:
      color - the color of the fill
      opacity - the opacity value between 0.0 (transparent) and 1.0 (opaque)
      Returns:
      a solid fill with the given color and opacity
      See Also:
      • solid(PotColor)

    • hex

      public static PotFill hex(String hex)
      Creates a solid fill from a hexadecimal color string.

      The string is parsed by PotColor.hex(String). The resulting fill has full opacity.

      Parameters:
      hex - the hexadecimal color string (e.g., "#FF0000" or "FF0000")
      Returns:
      a solid fill with the parsed color
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the hex string is invalid
      See Also:
      • PotColor.hex(String)

    • gradient

      public static PotFill gradient(PotGradient gradient)
      Creates a gradient fill.

      The resulting fill will have PotFill.Type.GRADIENT and full opacity.

      Parameters:
      gradient - the gradient definition
      Returns:
      a gradient fill

    • picture

      public static PotFill picture(byte[] imageData)
      Creates a picture fill from raw image data.

      The resulting fill will have PotFill.Type.PICTURE and full opacity. The image data is stored as-is; no validation or decoding is performed.

      Parameters:
      imageData - the raw bytes of the image
      Returns:
      a picture fill

    • pattern

      public static PotFill pattern(PotFill.PatternType patternType, PotColor foregroundColor, PotColor backgroundColor)
      Creates a pattern fill with the specified pattern type and colors.

      The resulting fill will have PotFill.Type.PATTERN and full opacity. The pattern uses the foreground color for the pattern elements and the background color for the base.

      Usage Example

      
 PotFill crossPattern = PotFill.pattern(PatternType.CROSS, PotColor.BLACK, PotColor.WHITE);
      Parameters:
      patternType - the preset pattern type, corresponding to OpenXML's prst attribute
      foregroundColor - the color used for the pattern elements
      backgroundColor - the color used for the pattern background
      Returns:
      a pattern fill

    • none

      public static PotFill none()
      Returns a fill representing no fill (transparent).

      This is equivalent to the constant NONE.

      Returns:
      a fill of type PotFill.Type.NONE
      See Also:
      • NONE

    • withOpacity

      public PotFill withOpacity(double opacity)
      Returns a new fill with the same properties but a different opacity.

      This method creates a copy of the current fill with the specified opacity value, which is clamped to the range [0.0, 1.0]. All other properties remain unchanged.

      Parameters:
      opacity - the new opacity value between 0.0 (transparent) and 1.0 (opaque)
      Returns:
      a new PotFill instance with the updated opacity

    • isNone

      public boolean isNone()
      Returns whether this fill is of type PotFill.Type.NONE.
      Returns:
      true if the fill type is NONE

    • isSolid

      public boolean isSolid()
      Returns whether this fill is of type PotFill.Type.SOLID.
      Returns:
      true if the fill type is SOLID

    • isGradient

      public boolean isGradient()
      Returns whether this fill is of type PotFill.Type.GRADIENT.
      Returns:
      true if the fill type is GRADIENT

    • isPicture

      public boolean isPicture()
      Returns whether this fill is of type PotFill.Type.PICTURE.
      Returns:
      true if the fill type is PICTURE

    • isImage

      public boolean isImage()
      Returns whether this fill is an image-based fill.

      This is equivalent to isPicture() and is provided for semantic clarity.

      Returns:
      true if the fill type is PICTURE
      See Also:
      • isPicture()

    • isPattern

      public boolean isPattern()
      Returns whether this fill is of type PotFill.Type.PATTERN.
      Returns:
      true if the fill type is PATTERN

    • getType

      public PotFill.Type getType()
      Returns the type of this fill.
      Returns:
      the fill type

    • getColor

      public PotColor getColor()
      Returns the color associated with this fill.

      For solid fills, this is the fill color. For pattern fills, this is the foreground color. For other types, this returns null.

      Returns:
      the color, or null if not applicable

    • getGradient

      public PotGradient getGradient()
      Returns the gradient definition for this fill.
      Returns:
      the gradient, or null if the fill is not a gradient

    • getImageData

      public byte[] getImageData()
      Returns the raw image data for this fill.
      Returns:
      the image bytes, or null if the fill is not a picture

    • getPatternType

      public PotFill.PatternType getPatternType()
      Returns the pattern type for this fill.
      Returns:
      the pattern type, or null if the fill is not a pattern

    • getBackgroundColor

      public PotColor getBackgroundColor()
      Returns the background color for a pattern fill.

      For pattern fills created via pattern(PatternType, PotColor, PotColor), this returns the specified background color. For all other fill types, this returns null.

      Returns:
      the background color for pattern fills, or null otherwise

    • getOpacity

      public double getOpacity()
      Returns the opacity of this fill as a double between 0.0 and 1.0.
      Returns:
      the opacity value

    • getOpacityPercent

      public int getOpacityPercent()
      Returns the opacity as an integer percentage scaled by 1000 (OpenXML format).

      This converts the 0.0-1.0 opacity to an integer in the range 0-100000, as used by OpenXML's alpha value representation.

      Returns:
      the opacity as an integer between 0 and 100000

    • equals

      public boolean equals(Object o)
      Compares this fill to another object for equality.

      Two fills are considered equal if they have the same type, opacity, and relevant data (color, gradient, pattern type). Image data is not compared directly.

      Overrides:
      equals in class Object
      Parameters:
      o - the object to compare with
      Returns:
      true if the objects are equal

    • hashCode

      public int hashCode()
      Returns a hash code value for this fill.
      Overrides:
      hashCode in class Object
      Returns:
      the hash code

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a string representation of this fill.

      The format is descriptive and varies by fill type, showing key properties.

      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      a string representation
